data.title: North Korea targets crypto workers with new info-stealing malware
data.description:
A North Korean-aligned threat actor has been targeting job seekers in the crypto industry with new malware that is designed to steal passwords for crypto wallets and password managers.

Cisco Talos reported on Wednesday that it found a new Python-based remote access trojan (RAT) it called “PylangGhost,” linking the malware to a North Korean-affiliated hacking collective called “Famous Chollima,” also known as “Wagemole.”

The hacking group has been targeting job seekers and employees with cryptocurrency and blockchain experience, primarily in India, with the attacks carried out through fake job interview campaigns using social engineering.

“Based on the advertised positions, it is clear that the Famous Chollima is broadly targeting individuals with previous experience in cryptocurrency and blockchain technologies.” 

Fake job sites and tests a cover for malware

The attackers create fraudulent job sites that impersonate legitimate companies, such as Coinbase, Robinhood and Uniswap, and victims are guided through a multi-step process. 

This includes initial contact from fake recruiters who send invites to skill-testing websites where the information gathering occurs.

Next, the victims are lured into enabling video and camera access for fake interviews during which they are tricked into copying and executing malicious commands under the pretense of installing updated video drivers, resulting in the compromise of their device. 

Payload targets crypto wallets 

PylangGhost is a variant of the previously documented GolangGhost RAT, and shares similar functionality, Cisco Talos said.

Upon execution, the commands enable remote control of the infected system and the theft of cookies and credentials from over 80 browser extensions, it reported. 

These include password managers and cryptocurrency wallets, including MetaMask, 1Password, NordPass, Phantom, Bitski, Initia, TronLink and MultiverseX. 

Multitasking malware 

The malware can carry out other tasks and execute numerous commands, including taking screenshots, managing files, stealing browser data, collecting system information and maintaining remote access to infected systems.

The researchers also noted that it was unlikely that the threat actors used an artificial intelligence large language model to help write the code, based on the comments made within it.

Fake job lures not new 

It is not the first time North Korean-linked hackers have used fake jobs and interviews to lure their victims. 

In April, hackers linked to the $1.4 billion Bybit heist were targeting crypto developers using fake recruitment tests infected with malware.

data.title: India's Sensex Opens Flat at 81354.85
Eutelsat Communications Mulls EUR1.35 Billion Capital Raise to Fund Medium-term Plan
data.description:
Eutelsat Communications said Thursday it is considering a capital raise of 1.35 billion euros to help finance the execution of its medium-term strategy.

The French satellite operator is looking to raise funds through a reserved capital increase of 716 million euros at 4 euros per share and a rights offering of 634 million euros. Completion is expected by the end of 2025.

The French state through Agence des Participations de l'Etat, Bharti Space, CMA, and Le Fonds Stratégique de Participations would subscribe for 526.4 million euros, 31.4 million euros, 100.4 million euros, and 57.8 million euros, respectively, under the reserved capital increase. The same investors would subscribe for their rights in the rights issue.

Following the transactions, the French state will own 29.99% of Eutelsat's capital and voting rights, while Bharti, CMA, and Le Fonds Stratégique will hold 18.70%, 7.81%, and 5.22%, respectively.

data.title: China 10-Year Yield Steady After PBOC Holds Rates
data.description:
China’s 10-year government bond yield held steady around 1.64% on Friday, as markets absorbed the latest policy decision from the People’s Bank of China.

The central bank left key lending rates unchanged during the June fixing, in line with market expectations.

The one-year loan prime rate (LPR) remained at a record low of 3%, while the five-year LPR also stayed at a record low of 3.5%.

This decision followed a 10 bps rate cut last month, aimed at easing economic pressure stemming from the potential impact of US tariffs, and came after major state-owned banks lowered deposit rates.

On the geopolitical front, US President Donald Trump has reportedly extended a final ultimatum to Iran to reach a settlement over its nuclear program, delaying any decision on potential military action for up to two weeks.

Meanwhile, China swiftly condemned Israel for its attack on Iran.

However, Beijing has stopped short of offering Iran any substantial support beyond maintaining regular trade ties.

data.title: Mozambique energy minister optimistic on TotalEnergies' plan to resume LNG project
data.description:
Mozambique's energy minister said on Friday the government has not received a request from TotalEnergies to lift a force majeure declaration on its $20-billion liquefied natural gas (LNG) project there, but he is optimistic about the oil major's plan to resume its development this summer.

The force majeure will be lifted as soon as the project's operator determines conditions are in place to resume operations, Minister of Mineral Resources and Energy Estevao Pale told reporters in Tokyo, after meeting with Japan's industry minister, Muto Yoji.

"We, as government, are doing everything that we can to be able to resume the project," Pale said.

"We are working together with all partners on the project to create the security conditions favourable to restart the project," he said, adding that security conditions have improved considerably.

TotalEnergies Chief Executive Patrick Pouyanne had said at an energy conference in Tokyo on Wednesday that he expects the project to resume development "this summer".

Covered by force majeure since 2021 following insurgent attacks, the Mozambique LNG project includes development of the Golfinho and Atum natural gas fields in the Offshore Area 1 concession and the building of a two-train liquefaction plant.

The plant will have an annual capacity of 13.12 million metric tons.

French oil and gas giant TotalEnergies is the project's operator with a stake of 26.5%, followed by Mitsui & Co with 20%. Mozambique's state-owned ENH has 15%, and Indian state companies and Thailand's PTTEP own the rest.

data.title: STO Express Logs 13% Rise in May Express Logistics Revenue
data.description:
STO Express posted a 13% year-over-year rise in express logistics services revenue in May to 4.41 billion yuan.

The logistics service provider made 2.26 billion deliveries last month, up 16% from a year earlier, according to a Friday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

Revenue per courier service declined 3.0% to 1.95 yuan.

Shares of the company rose 1% in recent trade.

data.title: ASX Midday Sector Update: Information Technology Stocks Soar, Financials Down
data.description:
Information technology stocks advanced 0.3% at midday Friday, with Brent crude edging 0.1% higher.

Investors remained on edge, and White House spokeswoman Karoline Leavitt said US President Donald Trump will decide within two weeks whether the US will strike Iran. Trading on Wall Street was halted because of a public holiday.

SOCO appointed Tristan Garthe as its interim chief financial officer and company secretary, while it searches for a long-term replacement CFO. Its CFO, Steve Parks, resigned earlier, and his last day at the firm will be July 31.

On the flip side, the consumer discretionary sector fell 1%.

Omni Bridgeway said it is actively working with the International Legal Finance Association (ILFA) to inform and steer the process surrounding a draft reconciliation version of the US Budget Bill, and based on current information, the firm considers it unlikely that the legal finance specific tax provision as included in the US Senate Finance Committee draft will ultimately be enacted into law.

data.title: Vietnam Q2 GDP growth estimated at 7.6% y/y, deputy PM says
data.description:
Vietnam's gross domestic product is expected to have grown 7.6% in the second quarter from a year earlier, Deputy Prime Minister Nguyen Hoa Binh told parliament on Friday.

Binh said annual growth for the first half is estimated at 7.3%, adding it would be a "big challenge" to reach this year's growth target of 8%.

He said Vietnam is seeking to maintain harmonious trade relations with the United States and China, its two largest trading partners.

data.title: Ringgit May Trade in 4.24-4.27 Range Vs. Dollar Next Week — Market Talk
data.description:
0336 GMT - The Malaysian ringgit is expected to trade between 4.24 and 4.27 against the dollar next week, as rising geopolitical tensions and tariff uncertainty keep the dollar supported, Kenanga economists say in a note. While higher oil prices offer short-term support for the dollar, long-term U.S. fiscal concerns could cap gains, they say. Market focus may remain on Middle East developments and the Fed's June 25 board meeting, which may influence capital flows, they add. Kenanga expects USD/MYR to face resistance at 4.26 and find support at 4.24 in the short term. USD/MYR is 0.1% lower at 4.2547. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0311 GMT - Thailand's exports face significant downside threat from U.S. tariff chaos, particularly in 2H, DBS senior economist Chua Han Teng says in a note. The 90-day reciprocal tariff pause spurred front-loading of orders and contributed to May's 18.4% growth in goods exports from a year earlier, he writes. However, the temporary boost would be followed by a significant payback through decelerating trade in 2H. "The outlook is complicated by heightened uncertainty surrounding negotiations between Thailand and the U.S. aimed at reducing U.S. tariffs from the 36% proposed on Liberation Day," Chua says. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0305 GMT - The South Korean government's proposed second extra budget is likely to prompt the Bank of Korea to raise its 2025 growth forecast to 1.0% from 0.8% at its August policy meeting, Nomura analysts say in a note. Fiscal stimulus is likely to ease growth concerns and strengthen the BOK's hawkish stance in monetary policy, they say. Nomura now expects no further rate cuts from the BOK this year and forecasts one last quarter-percentage-point cut in February 2026 for its current easing cycle. That should bring the base rate to 2.25% in South Korea. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0243 GMT - Thailand's potential prolonged political turmoil would undermine business confidence, DBS senior economist Chua Han Teng writes in a note. The Thai economy is already grappling with external headwinds from the U.S. tariff roller coaster, he says. "Domestic political uncertainty due to the exit of Bhumjaithai Party from Thailand's ruling coalition government is clouding an already fragile economic landscape," he adds. Bhumjaithai Party, the second-largest in the Pheu Thai-led ruling coalition, withdrew from the alliance earlier this week amid a lack of trust in Thai Prime Minister Paetongtarn Shinawatra, Chua notes. There was a leaked phone call with former Cambodian leader Hun Sen in which Paetongtarn criticized the Thai army. Bhumjaithai Party's withdrawal leaves the ruling coalition with a slim majority and little room for further exits, Chua says. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0229 GMT - Malaysia's sales tax expansion, effective July 1, is expected to generate 10 billion ringgit annually and support fiscal reform, RHB Investment Bank analyst Alexander Chia says in a note. While sectors like consumer, property and REITs may see slight pressure, targeted exemptions and sustained demand should limit earnings risk, he says. Chia sees room for the government to boost aid to lower-income groups and prefers defensive, domestic-centric stocks amid near-term trade and geopolitical overhangs. Additional revenue from sales tax could be used to stimulate the domestic economy during this period, he adds. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0156 GMT - Most Asian currencies are stronger against the dollar in early Asian trading. Traders are keeping an eye on escalating hostilities between Israel and Iran as well as potential U.S. strikes on the Islamic Republic. President Trump says he will decide on whether to strike Iran "within two weeks," according to the White House. "The U.S. dollar is still the reigning world reserve currency, but of late has not been the most effective hedge against volatility," says John Sidawi, senior portfolio manager for international fixed income at Federated Hermes. USD/KRW is 0.4% lower at 1,370.70. USD/MYR is down 0.2% at 4.25. USD/SGD is 0.2% lower at 1.28. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0048 GMT - Japanese government bonds are under selling pressure following signs of persistent domestic inflation. The government said earlier that Japan's consumer price index, excluding fresh food, rose 3.7% in May from a year earlier, compared with April's3.5% increase and expectations for a 3.6% rise. Persistently high inflation is keeping market expectations intact for the Bank of Japan rate increases down the road, despite the economic uncertainty stemming from U.S. tariffs and the Middle East conflict. Ten-year JGB futures are 0.11 yen lower at Y139.22. The 20-year JGB yield is up half a basis point at 2.365%. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

0036 GMT - While Australia's May employment data saw a fall in jobs growth, the data is unlikely to shift the dial for the Reserve Bank of Australia, which considers the job market to be relatively tight, says Westpac economist Ryan Wells. Westpac remains comfortable with its view that the RBA's next rate cut is most likely to occur in August, he adds. The RBA is cognizant of the downside risks to global and domestic growth, but has also made it clear it wants to adjust policy in a cautious and predictable manner. (james.glynn@wsj.com; @JamesGlynnWSJ)

0026 GMT - The Philippine central bank is expected to continue cutting rates this year, with inflation set to stay low, Capital Economics senior Asia economist Gareth Leather writes in a note. BSP's decision to cut its policy rate on Thursday came as no surprise. The dovish tone from the central bank's statement and press conference suggests further easing is likely, he adds. "The economy could certainly do with a boost," he says. Sequential 1Q GDP growth slowed sharply. Low inflation and falling interest rates will also offer some support to demand this year, he says. Capital Economics expects another 50 bp cut from the BSP by year-end.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0019 GMT - Japan's Nikkei Stock Average is up 0.2% at 38577.17 as gains in chip stocks help offset losses in real-estate and heavy-industry shares. Kioxia Holdings gains 5.6% and Advantest is 2.2% higher, while Mitsui Fudosan falls 1.1% and IHI is 2.2% lower. Broader market index Topix is down 0.1% at 2790.44. USD/JPY is at 145.26, little changed from 145.25 as of Thursday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are focusing on any developments related to the Israel-Iran conflict and its implications for energy prices. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

0004 GMT - South Korea's fiscal stimulus could lift 2025 growth by 0.3 percentage point, Goldman Sachs economists Irene Choi and Goohoon Kwon write in a note. The government's latest second supplementary budget proposal that entails a KRW14.9 trillion net increase in expenditures could add 0.1 percentage point to headline GDP growth this year, on top of the 0.2 percentage point added by the first extra-budget package, they project. South Korea's economy has been forecast to grow 0.8% in 2025, compared with 2024's 2.0% growth, according to the latest forecasts from the Bank of Korea and state think tank Korea Development Institute, which have recently downgraded their 2025 growth outlooks. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

2342 GMT - Japanese stocks may trade rangebound as uncertainty continues over the Israel-Iran conflict and its implications for energy prices. Nikkei futures are flat at 38510 on the SGX. USD/JPY is at 145.30, compared with 145.25 as of Thursday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are closely watching any developments in the Middle East. The Nikkei Stock Average fell 1.0% to 38488.34 on Thursday. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com)

data.title: Iron Ore Rises; Likely To Trade Rangebound in Near Term — Market Talk
data.description: Iron ore rises in early Asian trade. Prices show a slight rebound while there is still downward pressure from high shipments and accumulating inventory at ports, analysts at Nanhua Futures write in a note. Meanwhile, profit from steel mills is rising, with room to increase production in the short-term, supporting demand, they add. Prices are likely to trade rangebound for now, they say. The most-traded iron-ore contract on the Dalian Commodity Exchange is up 1.2% at CNY705.0 a ton. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

data.title: Future Metals Says Entitlement Offer to Raise Around AU$2.6 Million Closes; Shares Fall 12%
data.description:
Future Metals said that its one-for-three pro-rata non-renounceable entitlement offer to raise up to around AU$2.6 million closed on June 13, and it received valid acceptances from eligible shareholders for 190.1 million new shares, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

The offer was fully underwritten by CPS Capital Group, which will take up 26.5 million new shares, and Zeta Resources, which will take up 23 million new shares.

Zeta will hold an interest of 94.8 million shares in the firm, representing a 9.9% stake.

Future Metals applied to the London Stock Exchange for admission to trading on AIM for the 239.6 million new shares, expected to occur on or around June 23.

The shares fell nearly 12% in recent trading on Friday.

data.title: Shanghai Kai Kai to Subscribe for 20 Million Yuan Wealth Management Products
data.description:
Shanghai Kai Kai Industry plans to subscribe for wealth management products worth 20 million yuan, according to a Shanghai bourse disclosure on Friday.

The Chinese textile products manufacturer will apply for structured deposits at Bank of Ningbo .

The product has an expected annualized return of between 1% and 2%, with a yield of between 98,600 yuan and 197,300 yuan. It has a term of 180 days, maturing on Dec. 17.

data.title: New World Says Central Asia Sweetens Takeover Offer; Shares Rise 6%
data.description:
New World Resources said UK-based mining company Central Asia Metals (CAML), raised its offer to AU$0.053 per share from AU$0.050, according to a Tuesday filing with the Australian bourse.

The revised offer represents an 89.3% premium to the company's last closing price of AU$0.028 on May 20, the filing said.

The board recommends that shareholders vote in favor of the scheme in the absence of a superior proposal and subject to an expert's opinion that the offer is in the best interest of the shareholders.

CAML agreed to provide the company with interim funding of AU$10 million via the issue of around 188.7 million shares at the same price as the improved takeover offer, the filing added.

New World Resources said the placement is conditional on no superior offer emerging within 14 days of the agreement.

The scheme meeting is set for mid-September, the company added.

The company has advised shareholders not to take any action on the proposal at this time.

Shares of the company rose 6% in recent Friday trade.

data.title: Specialized Medical Co. Finalizes Investor Offering Under Saudi IPO
data.description:
Saudi Arabian hospital operator Specialized Medical Co. finalized the individual investor offering period under its initial public offering on the Saudi Exchange.

The offer, which started Sunday and closed Monday, registered a demand of 542.2 million Saudi riyals from 317,820 subscribers and comprised an allocation of 15 million shares, according to a Thursday release.

Each retail investor will receive at least 10 shares, while the remaining shares are to be distributed on a pro-rata basis, with an average allocation factor of 63.9%.

The offer shares will be listed on the main market of the Saudi Exchange, subject to completion of listing formalities with the Capital Market Authority and the bourse.

data.title: SF Holding Logs 11% Rise in May Revenue; Shares Up 4%
data.description:
SF Holding recorded a total revenue of 25.1 billion yuan in May, up 11% from 22.6 billion yuan a year prior.

Express logistics revenue rose 13% year over year to 19.4 billion yuan from 17.1 billion yuan, according to a Friday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

Supply chain and international revenue grew 5.0% to 5.73 billion yuan from 5.46 billion yuan.

Shares of the logistics company rose 4% in recent trade on the Shenzhen and Hong Kong bourses.

data.title: Yunda Holding's Express Logistics Revenue Rises 7% in May
data.description:
Yunda Holding's revenue from express logistics services climbed 6.8% to 4.42 billion yuan in May from a year earlier, according to a Friday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

The logistics service provider delivered 2.30 billion parcels during the same month, up 13% from a year earlier.

Revenue per courier service slipped 5.4% to 1.92 yuan.

data.title: Hanyang Securities Gets New Largest Shareholder
data.description:
KCGI Private Equity Investment Partnership became the largest shareholder of Hanyang Securities , replacing a consortium comprising Hanyang Academy and seven others.

KCGI currently holds 3,766,973 shares of Hanyang Securities, equivalent to a 29.59% stake, the South Korean capital market company said in a Friday filing with the Korea Exchange.

Shares of the company fell nearly 2% in recent trade on Friday.

data.title: BayWa Wins Approval for UK Solar Farm Project
data.description:
BayWa's UK solar farm project won a development consent decision from Britain's Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero on Thursday, according to a same-day statement.

The Oaklands Farm Solar Park project will span 400 acres, with a solar power generation capacity of 138 megawatts and energy storage capacity of up to 37.5 megawatts, according to the German agricultural and industrial group's website.

data.title: Stamford Land Appoints Acting CFO
data.description:
Stamford Land appointed Lau Yin Whai as the company's acting chief financial officer, effective June 16, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange late Thursday.

Whai succeeds Ang Lay Khen, who will leave her CFO post on Sept. 16 due to personal reasons.

Shares of the company were up over 1% in recent trading.

data.title: Constellation announces the approval of its share reverse split at the Extraordinary General Meeting and provides key information regarding its implementation
data.description:
Luxembourg, June 20th, 2025 - Reference is made to the notice of anExtraordinary General Meeting disclosed on 13 May 2025 (the "EGM") byConstellation Oil Services Holding S.A. (together with its subsidiaries,"Constellation" or the "Company") and the minutes from the EGM published onJune19 2025, where it was proposed to carry out a share reverse split of theCompany's registered ordinary shares (the "Shares") in the ratio 18:1. Acorresponding reverse split in the ratio of 18:1 will be completed for theCompany's listed NDRs.

Below, the main information and deadlines in relation to the reverse sharesplitof the Norwegian Depositary Receipts ("NDRs"):

- Date on which the corporate action was made public: 13 May 2025 (noticeof EGMpublished).- Reverse split ratio: 18 old NDRs grouped in 1 new NDR.- Last day including right (last trading day before reverse split): 4July. - Ex-date: 7 July. - Record date: 8 July.- New ISIN: NO0013597419.- Date of ISIN change: 7 July. - Rounding rules: individual holders of an amount of NDRs which after thereverse split, thus after it is divided by 18, results in fractional shares,will have their holdings of NDRs rounded down to the nearest whole number.Therefore, fractional NDRs will not be issued, and Company's Board ofDirectorswill ensure that the eventual fractional NDRs are grouped and sold on EuronextGrowth Oslo, with net proceeds from the sale being donated for charity.- Date of approval: 19 June 2025 (EGM held and minutes published).

The company recommends that NDR holders who wish to avoid being rounded downdueto fractional amounts resulting from the reverse split, adjust their holdings,by buying or selling NDRs, so that their total NDR holdings are exactlydivisible by 18, leaving no remaining fractions. Such trades must be doneuntilJuly 4, 2025 (included).

The company recommends that persons who hold Shares directly (i.e., not viaNDRslisted on Oslo Euronext Growth) and who wish to avoid being rounded down duetofractional amounts resulting from the reverse split, adjust their holdings, bybuying or selling Shares, so that their total Share holdings are exactlydivisible by 18, leaving no remaining fractions. Such transfers must be doneandduly notified to the Company together with the corresponding documentation atir@theconstellation.com until July 4, 2025 (included).

This information is published in accordance with the requirements of theContinuing Obligations for companies listed on the Oslo Stock Exchange andEuronext Expand.

For further information, please contact ir@theconstellation.com

https://newsweb.oslobors.no/message/649714

data.title: GfK: UK Consumer Confidence Improves in June
data.description:
Britain's consumer confidence indicator rose to -18 points in June from -20 points in May, market research company Growth from Knowledge said Friday.

Analysts expected -20 points for the month.

GfK noted improvements in the measures for the consumers' perception of the general economy, with overall sentiment remaining cautious due to persistent global tariff disputes and geopolitical tensions across the Middle East.

data.title: SNG: World Bank projects Qatar's economy to grow at an average of 6.5% in 2026-2027
data.description:
First published: 20-Jun-2025 07:27:50

Staff Writer

Riyadh: The World Bank projected that the economic growth in the State of Qatar is to remain stable at 2.4% in 2025, before accelerating to an average of 6.5% in 2026-2027 due to the expansion of LNG capacity.

These improved prospects are supported by strong non-hydrocarbon growth, particularly in education, tourism, and services, the World Bank said in its report "Gulf Economic Update."

The hydrocarbon sector is expected to growth timidly in 2025 (0.9%), before undergoing a significant boost in 2026 thanks to the North Field LNG expansion coming online, supporting a 40% rise in LNG output. Non-hydrocarbon growth is expected to remain robust thanks to infrastructure upgrades and international investments, the report said.

"Economic growth across the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) is projected to increase in the medium-term to 3.2% in 2025 and 4.50% in 2026. This growth is likely to be driven by the expected rollback of OPEC+ oil production cuts and robust expansion of non-oil sectors," according to the report.

According to the latest edition of the report, regional growth was 1.7% in 2024 - an improvement from 0.3% in 2023. The non-hydrocarbon sector remained resilient, expanding by 3.7% - largely fueled by private consumption, investment, and structural reforms across the GCC.

At the same time, global trade uncertainty presents challenges, as a global economic slowdown remains a key downside risk for the region. To mitigate these risks, GCC countries need to accelerate economic diversification reforms and strengthen regional trade.

"The resilience of GCC countries in navigating global uncertainties while advancing economic diversification underscores their strong commitment to long-term prosperity," Division Director for the GCC countries at the World Bank Safaa El Tayeb El-Kogali said.

"Strategic fiscal policies, targeted investments, and a strong focus on innovation, entrepreneurship, and job creation for youth are essential to sustaining growth and stability," she added.

data.title: Odyssey Gold Raises AU$4 Million via Share Placement; Shares Fall 13%
data.description:
Odyssey Gold received commitments to raise AU$4 million through the placement of 222.2 million shares at AU$0.018 apiece, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The placement also comes with one free attaching option for every two shares subscribed for, exercisable at AU$0.036 apiece, expiring two years from issue, the filing said.

Directors of the company will subscribe for 16.9 million worth of shares to raise AU$300,000, subject to shareholder approval.

Proceeds will be used to advance works at the Tuckanarra project in Western Australia, per the filing.

Argonaut Securities and Taylor Collision were the joint lead managers for the placement.

Shares fell 13% in midday trade Friday.

data.title: Australia's Minbos Resources rises as construction progresses at Angola facility
data.description:
** Shares of fertilizer distributor Minbos Resources rise 9.8% to A$0.045; on track for best session since June 3, if gains hold

** Co advances construction of its Subantando fertilizer plant in Africa's Angola by approving order of items including diesel storage and pumping system, power line connection, etc.

** Says completing initial stage construction activities at the facility

** Shares set to snap five-session losing streak

** Stock down 23.7% YTD

data.title: Dalata Hotel Gets Competition Regulator's Nod to Buy Irish Hotel
data.description:
Dalata Hotel Group (DHG.IR) secured approval from Ireland's Competition and Consumer Protection Commission to purchase the Radisson Blu Hotel Dublin Airport.

The deal, which includes buying the property's long leasehold interest holder, CG Hotels Dublin Airport, for 83 million euros, is anticipated to close before the end of June, the independent hotel operator said Thursday.

data.title: US Tariffs, Global Trade Uncertainty Drive BoJ's Caution on Japan's Economy, May Minutes of Meeting Show
data.description:
The Bank of Japan (BoJ) highlighted rising downside risks to Japan's economy and prices due to uncertainties surrounding global trade policies, particularly US tariff measures, according to its May minutes of meeting.

On Tuesday, BoJ members agreed to maintain the uncollateralized overnight call rate at around 0.5%, emphasizing the need for accommodative financial conditions to support the economy.

Many members stressed the importance of closely monitoring the impact of trade policies on Japan's exports, corporate profits, and household sentiment.

Some members noted that real interest rates remain significantly low, which is necessary to sustain economic recovery amid heightened uncertainties.

One member advocated for a wait-and-see approach until US trade policy developments stabilize, given their potential to disrupt Japan's economic outlook.

Concerns were raised that US tariffs could pressure Japanese firms to cut costs excessively, restrain wages, and reduce investment, risking industrial hollowing-out.

Members agreed that if economic and price conditions improve as projected, the BoJ should continue raising interest rates gradually to adjust monetary accommodation.

Several members supported further rate hikes, citing the likelihood of achieving the 2% inflation target, albeit with a delay due to trade-related risks.

One member downplayed the risk of inflation expectations reversing, noting that underlying price trends remain on track toward the target.

The BoJ emphasized flexibility, with one member suggesting a temporary pause in rate hikes but readiness to act again depending on US policy shifts and domestic economic developments.

data.title: KALENDER-Termine Politik/Vermischtes am Freitag, 20. Juni
data.description:
*************************************************************

Weitere ausführliche Terminübersichten erhalten sie unter:

* FIRMEN/MÄRKTE

* KONJUNKTUR/GELDPOLITIK

*************************************************************

Alle Zeitangaben in Central European Time (CET)

Macron visits 55th Paris Air Show

French President Emmanuel Macron visits the 55th Paris Air Show at Le Bourget Airport. The show gathers hundreds of exhibitors in the aerospace and defence industry, and focuses on supply chain challenges, new aircraft orders and tariffs.

New Zealand prime minister visits China

New Zealand’s Prime Minister Christopher Luxon will visit China from June 17-20, meeting with President Xi Jinping and Premier Li Qiang.

Japan Energy Summit

Japan Energy Summit & Exhibition takes place from June 18 to 20 in Tokyo, co-hosted by Tokyo Gas and JERA.

09:00 - LNG Producer Consumer Conference

The 14th LNG Producer Consumer Conference is co-hosted by the Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry (METI) and the IEA and in collaboration with the Japan Energy Summit & Exhibition.

11:00 - Mattei Plan for Africa and the Global Gateway' summit held in Rome

Italy's PM Giorgia Meloni and European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen chair "The Mattei Plan for Africa and the Global Gateway: A common effort with the African Continent" summit in Rome

12:00 - Taiwan election commission to announce date of recall elections for more than a dozen lawmakers

13:00 - Putin speaks at St.Petersburg International Economic Forum

Russian President Vladimir Putin speaks at plenary session of the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum (SPIEF). Other speakers will include President of Indonesia Prabowo Subianto, Vice Premier of China Ding Xuexiang, Vice President of South Africa Paul Mashatile, National Security Advisor of Bahrain Shaikh Nasser bin Hamad and OPEC General Secretary Haitham Al Ghais.

data.title: China's rare earth magnet shipments halve in May due to export curbs
data.description:
China's overseas shipments of rare earth magnets halved in May from April, tumbling to their lowest levels in more than five years due to export curbs.

Beijing said this month that it would speed up its approval process, a concession made after the U.S. and China agreed to dial back trade tensions. In the meantime, however, industry sources say Chinese customs officials have become increasingly cautious about processing rare earth cargoes.

This is particularly so for rare earth magnets because there is only a single code to cover magnets despite there being a variety of chemistries, said the sources who were not authorised to speak to media and declined to be identified.

China, the world's largest rare earth magnet producer accounting for over 90% of global supply, decided in early April to impose restrictions on exports of seven medium-to-heavy rare earth products and some magnets. That's rocked supply chains crucial to auto, aerospace, semiconductor and military equipment sectors around the world.

Last month, China shipped out 1,238 metric tons of rare earth permanent magnets, down 52.9% from April and the lowest level for a single month since February 2020, data from the General Administration of Customs showed on Friday.

April's shipments had also halved from March.

On a year-on-year basis, May shipments were down 74%.

Sources have also previously said that customs are holding up some shipments of lower-performance rare earth magnets used in appliances and consumer electronics because of confusion over how to apply Beijing's export curbs.

China's commerce ministry said on Thursday that "a certain number" of rare earth export licence applications had been approved but did not disclose details.

Chinese rare earth magnet producers JL MAG Rare-Earth and Innuovo Technology announced in recent weeks that they have secured a few export licenses for some clients.

Exports of rare earth magnets for January-May slipped 14.5% from the same period last year to 19,132 tons, the lowest for the period since 2021.

data.title: US Masters Residential Property Fund Strengthens Position With $40 Million Fund Repatriation
data.description:
US Masters Residential Property Fund's board confirmed the successful repatriation of $40 million in cash from the US-based fund or US Masters Residential Property (US) Fund to the parent fund or US Masters Residential Property Fund, effective June 20, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The fund's shares were up almost 3% in recent Friday trade.
